questions.question: Explain the importance of statistics in education.
questions.answer: Statistics plays an important role in education by helping in evaluation of student performance, curriculum planning and educational research.

questions.question: Explain the different types of data with examples.
questions.answer: Data can be classified as discrete and continuous. Discrete data has countable values while continuous data can take any value within a range.

questions.question: Explain frequency distribution and its need.
questions.answer: Frequency distribution arranges data into classes with frequencies, making large data easy to understand and analyse.

questions.question: Describe the construction of a histogram.
questions.answer: A histogram is constructed by taking class intervals on the x-axis and frequencies on the y-axis using adjacent rectangles.

questions.question: Explain frequency polygon and its uses.
questions.answer: A frequency polygon is obtained by joining midpoints of class intervals and is useful for comparing distributions.

questions.question: Explain cumulative frequency distribution.
questions.answer: Cumulative frequency distribution shows the cumulative totals of frequencies and helps in locating median and quartiles.

questions.question: Explain the steps to find mean by direct method.
questions.answer: In direct method, mean is calculated using Σfx / Σf by multiplying class marks with frequencies.

questions.question: Explain assumed mean method for finding mean.
questions.answer: Assumed mean method simplifies calculations by taking a convenient value as mean and finding deviations.

questions.question: Explain step deviation method.
questions.answer: Step deviation method reduces calculations further by dividing deviations by class width.

questions.question: Explain median of grouped data.
questions.answer: Median of grouped data is found using cumulative frequency and median class formula.

questions.question: Explain mode of grouped data.
questions.answer: Mode is calculated using a formula based on modal class and neighbouring frequencies.

questions.question: Explain bar diagram with its types.
questions.answer: Bar diagrams represent discrete data and include simple, multiple and component bars.

questions.question: Explain ogive and its significance.
questions.answer: Ogive is a cumulative frequency curve used to determine median graphically.

questions.question: Explain tabulation of data.
questions.answer: Tabulation arranges data systematically into rows and columns for clarity.

questions.question: Explain classification of data.
questions.answer: Classification groups data into homogeneous categories for easy analysis.

questions.question: Explain population and sample with examples.
questions.answer: Population includes all items under study, while sample is a subset of the population.

questions.question: Explain frequency density and its application.
questions.answer: Frequency density is used when class widths are unequal in histograms.

questions.question: Explain measures of central tendency.
questions.answer: Measures of central tendency include mean, median and mode.

questions.question: Explain statistical investigation.
questions.answer: Statistical investigation involves collection, organisation, analysis and interpretation of data.

questions.question: Explain the need for data presentation.
questions.answer: Data presentation helps in better understanding and comparison of data.

questions.question: Explain statistical average with examples.
questions.answer: Statistical average represents the central value of data such as mean, median and mode.

questions.question: Explain range as a measure of dispersion.
questions.answer: Range measures dispersion by finding difference between highest and lowest values.

questions.question: Explain observation and variable.
questions.answer: Observation is each data value and variable is a quantity that varies.

questions.question: Explain data interpretation.
questions.answer: Data interpretation involves drawing meaningful conclusions from data.

questions.question: Explain misrepresentation of data.
questions.answer: Misrepresentation occurs when data is presented inaccurately leading to wrong conclusions.
